     The Numbers Business Report

Enchanted (2007)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $127,706,877Details
International Box Office $212,677,264
Worldwide Box Office $340,384,141
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $93,732,649
Total $93,732,649
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A classic Disney fairy tale collides with modern-day New York City in a story about a fairytale princess from the land of Andalasia who is thrust into the heart of New York City by an evil queen. Soon after her arrival, Princess Giselle begins to change her views on life and love after meeting a handsome lawyer. Can a storybook view of romance survive in the real world?

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$34,440,317 (27.0% of total gross)
Legs:3.71 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:37.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$85,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,730 opening theaters/3,730 max. theaters, 6.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $220,871,718

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 21st, 2007 (Wide) by Walt Disney
International Releases: December 20th, 2007 (Wide) (New Zealand)
November 12th, 2020 (Wide) (Australia)
July 6th, 2023 (Limited) (New Zealand)
Video Release: March 18th, 2008 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some scary images and mild innuendo
Running Time: 108 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animated Sequences
Geographical setting: New York, New York City
Plot point: Alternative Dimensions / Parallel universe, Dysfunctional Family, Prince/Princess, Romance, Step-Family
Subgenre: Family Adventure, Musical-Adventure
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Fantasy
Production/Financing Companies: Walt Disney Pictures, Barry Sonnenfeld, Josephson Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A classic Disney fairy tale collides with modern-day New York City in a story about a fairytale princess from the land of Andalasia who is thrust into the heart of New York City by an evil queen. Soon after her arrival, Princess Giselle begins to change her views on life and love after meeting a handsome lawyer. Can a storybook view of romance survive in the real world?
